narrative.what_this_demonstrates: A high-criticality e-commerce platform with a clear bimodal weekday pattern: heavy use during peak hours, very light use off-peak and on weekends. Currently provisioned at peak capacity 24/7. Scheduled scaling would dramatically reduce off-peak cost without affecting peak SLA.
narrative.why_this_scenario_exists: Tests cost-vs-reliability trade-off reasoning. The system must recognize that the cost problem is real and the fix preserves reliability — neither blind cost-cutting nor blanket "this is fine."
narrative.what_the_agent_should_conclude: Implement scheduled scaling to reduce capacity by 65% during off-peak hours and weekends while maintaining full capacity during peak windows.
narrative.what_distinguishes_good_from_great: A good recommendation says "scale down off-peak." A great recommendation quantifies the specific off-peak hours, weekends, and percentage capacity reduction, and articulates why this preserves the 99.99% SLA during the peak windows that matter.

before_after_evidence.config_before: m5.xlarge × 20 fixed capacity, db.r6g.2xlarge + 2 replicas, no scheduled scaling
before_after_evidence.config_after: Scheduled scaling: peak capacity 9–11 AM and 2–4 PM weekdays only; 65% capacity reduction otherwise
before_after_evidence.observed_outcome_summary: Peak-window SLA stayed at 99.99%. Off-peak cost dropped 65%. Weekends similarly reduced. Total monthly cost dropped by approximately $10,400.
before_after_evidence.source_attribution: Pattern observed across e-commerce-platform cohort during Q4 2025.
